select_when: External rules, enforcement priorities, or policy narratives are moving early enough that waiting for certainty would create strategic lag.
Weak signals need to be converted into scenarios, ownership questions, and preparatory options before the pressure becomes immediate.
Leaders must connect long-horizon regulatory shifts to present-day product, operating, or capital decisions.
The organization needs structured anticipation, not reactive compliance after the fact.
avoid_when: Horizon scanning has become a trend-slide ritual without thresholds, response triggers, or accountable owners.
The team is overreacting to headlines that have not yet become structurally meaningful.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on anticipatory monitoring, scenario conversion, and preparation triggers rather than on generic trend watching.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 4 or import donor-shaped higher-order semantics in this coverage batch.
Kept the routing surface conservative so Layer 2B stays canonical and Waves 2 to 4 stay preview-only.
open_questions: Wave 4 candidacy stays observational only for this batch.

select_when.note: Select conditions stay close to the source activation language and are compressed only enough for routing use.

avoid_when.note: Avoid conditions preserve the source anti-patterns and misuse warnings with conservative normalization.

input_type.note: The typed input label is a reviewed routing compression of the source context.

output_type.note: The typed output label compresses the intervention shape implied by the raw source.

reasoning_types.note: Reasoning taxonomy labels are normalized onto the existing Layer 2B vocabulary.
